_README: Global settings for the checks and guards. Copy to `checks-config.json` at the repo root and adjust. Every key is OPTIONAL — set only what you change; an absent file or key means the defaults below (built into the scripts, today's behavior). A present-but-broken file is a BLOCKING finding (CFG-INVALID), never silently ignored. Method-calibration constants (index-eval scorer/sufficiency thresholds) are deliberately NOT here: they define what the metric means and are not per-project taste.
_audit: When the deterministic report (`checks/decisions-audit.py --report`) recommends spending a tier-2 (LLM) audit, and how it batches. `volume-alert` = entry count per channel at which the channel is worth auditing; `pending-*` = ratification-inbox nudges; `pending-stale-days` also drives the stale marker of `memory-audit.py --pending`.
_sizes: Granularity signals (TO-CONFIRM, never blocking): an oversized entry is a hint that its content belongs in the durable doc, not a fault. Units: lines of useful content, words for labels.
_guards: EXTENSION-ONLY, fail-closed: a repo-local config can ADD surveillance (extra instruction files for the poisoning scan, extra path-regex allowlist entries for the secret scan) but can never disable a guard or remove a built-in pattern — otherwise poisoned content could switch off the alarm by editing this file. An invalid entry (bad regex) is skipped, the guard keeps running.
_doc-refs: R-DEAD-PATH tuning for `checks/doc-refs-check.py`. `ignore-prefixes` = prefixes of tokens that LOOK like repo paths but never are — typically a runtime API joined to a filename (`Application.persistentDataPath/save.txt` in a Unity project) or a machine-local path cited in a guide. Additive and narrow: a listed prefix silences R-DEAD-PATH for matching tokens only, nothing else.
_memory-graph: Settings for `hooks/memory-graph.py`. `channels-base` = the subdir the four memory channels live under, for a project that nests them (e.g. "Docs" → Docs/decisions/…); "" (default) = repo root, the framework's own layout. `self-extra-dirs` = tooling dirs to also self-suppress nudges on (default ["checks","hooks","adapters"]; a Claude Code project might use [".claude"]). `class-file-extensions` = extensions whose basename equals a source symbol (one-symbol-per-file), e.g. [".cs"]; empty (default) → covers matches by cited-path containment only, fully language-agnostic. `code-roots` = dirs the class/tag ambiguity guard scans (default: index-config roots, else the repo).
